params.name: Saturn V Model
params.description:
On Nov 9, 1967 AD, the Saturn V successfully conducted a test flight at the Kennedy Space Center.
The Saturn V is a super heavy-lift rocket and the largest rocket ever built by humans. It was the rocket that launched the Apollo 13 spacecraft to the moon. In the 1980s, NASA launched 13 Saturn V rockets, all of which were successful, making it a perfect launch record. However, due to its enormous size and high resource consumption, the Saturn V has since been retired.
